headline: Boeing’s Big Order From China Isn’t Big Enough—and the Stock Is Paying for It
summary: President Donald Trump’s summit with China’s leader Xi Jinping might yield some long-awaited business for  Boeing  but not enough business for the stock.  Boeing stock dropped 4.7% on Thursday and slid another 1.3% in Friday’s premarket.  Trump told Fox News on Thursday that a 200-plane order from China could be coming.

headline: Trump Says China Will Buy Over 200 Boeing Planes
summary: President Donald Trump says the US made great trade deals with China during his meeting with Xi Jinping. He says China will buy over 200 planes from Boeing and possibly 750 more, but he provided no specifics. Trump also said China is buying engines from General Electric. He spoke to reporters on Air Force One Friday.

content.title: Trump Says China Will Buy Over 200 Boeing Planes
content.description: President Donald Trump says the US made great trade deals with China during his meeting with Xi Jinping. He says China will buy over 200 planes from Boeing and possibly 750 more, but he provided no specifics. Trump also said China is buying engines from General Electric. He spoke to reporters on Air Force One Friday.
content.summary: President Donald Trump says the US made great trade deals with China during his meeting with Xi Jinping. He says China will buy over 200 planes from Boeing and possibly 750 more, but he provided no specifics. Trump also said China is buying engines from General Electric. He spoke to reporters on Air Force One Friday.

headline: BAE Systems delivers next-generation flight hardware for U.S. Space Force missile warning program
summary: BAE Systems (LON: BA) has delivered the sensor subassembly and sensor system controller components for the Next Generation Overhead Persistent Infrared Polar (NGP) program. The program will provide the U.S. Space Force with advanced missile warning, technical intelligence and battlespace characterization mission capabilities.

content.title: BAE Systems delivers next-generation flight hardware for U.S. Space Force missile warning program
content.summary: BAE Systems (LON: BA) has delivered the sensor subassembly and sensor system controller components for the Next Generation Overhead Persistent Infrared Polar (NGP) program. The program will provide the U.S. Space Force with advanced missile warning, technical intelligence and battlespace characterization mission capabilities.

thumbnail.caption: The BAE Systems sensor subassembly is a significant component of the mission payload and provides the optics, pointing mechanism, controlling electronics and electrical bus interface to the spacecraft for the Next Gen OPIR Polar program. (Credit: Northrop Grumman)

headline: Trump says China will buy 200 planes from Boeing, with a possibility of expanding the deal to 750
summary: Aircraft manufacturer Boeing will make its first major sale to China in nearly a decade with an order for 200 planes, President Donald Trump told reporters on Air Force One on Friday, a deal he said had been made during his summit with China's President Xi Jinping this week and that could grow to as many as 750 planes.  The White House has not released details of the deal, and the company itself has not commented.

content.title: Trump says China will buy 200 planes from Boeing, with a possibility of expanding the deal to 750
content.summary: Aircraft manufacturer Boeing will make its first major sale to China in nearly a decade with an order for 200 planes, President Donald Trump told reporters on Air Force One on Friday, a deal he said had been made during his summit with China's President Xi Jinping this week and that could grow to as many as 750 planes.  The White House has not released details of the deal, and the company itself has not commented.

thumbnail.caption: A Boeing 737 MAX airplane on the final assembly line is seen during a media tour at Boeing's factory Wednesday, April 15, 2026, in Renton, Wash. (AP Photo/Lindsey Wasson)

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Intel, Boeing, Coinbase, Coherent, Microsoft, Cerebras, Figma, Ford, Applied Materials, and More
summary: Stocks were falling Friday, pulling back from record levels, amid concerns over rising Treasury yields and the summit between President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping ending without any clear messaging of what is to come next.  The inference chip maker closed the session with a market capitalization of $66.95 billion, well above the valuation expected before its initial public offering priced on Wednesday.  Investors may have been taking the opportunity to lock in some profit: Heading into Friday’s session, shares were up 71% in 2026.

headline: Trump says China agreed to buy 200 Boeing planes and signaled interest in as many as 750
summary: Aircraft manufacturer Boeing will make its first major sale to China in nearly a decade with an order for 200 planes, President Donald Trump told reporters Friday as he flew back from his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.  Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said China reserved the right to buy as many as 750 Boeing aircraft as part of a deal reached during the summit.  The White House did not specify the types of planes or provide any other details.

content.title: Trump says China agreed to buy 200 Boeing planes and signaled interest in as many as 750
content.summary: Aircraft manufacturer Boeing will make its first major sale to China in nearly a decade with an order for 200 planes, President Donald Trump told reporters Friday as he flew back from his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.  Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said China reserved the right to buy as many as 750 Boeing aircraft as part of a deal reached during the summit.  The White House did not specify the types of planes or provide any other details.

thumbnail.caption: A Boeing 737 MAX airplane on the final assembly line is seen during a media tour at Boeing's factory Wednesday, April 15, 2026, in Renton, Wash. (AP Photo/Lindsey Wasson)

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Intel, Boeing, Cerebras, Microsoft, DexCom, Figma, Coherent, Coinbase, and More
summary: Stocks were falling Friday, pulling back from record levels, as oil prices were rising amid concerns over rising Treasury yields and the summit between President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping coming to an end without any clear messaging of what is next and no meaningful conclusion on the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.  The inference chip maker closed the session with a market capitalization of $66.95 billion, well above the valuation expected before its initial public offering priced on Wednesday.  Investors may have been taking the opportunity to lock in some profit: Heading into Friday’s session, shares were up 71% in 2026.

The inference chip maker closed the session with a market capitalization of $66.95 billion, well above the valuation expected before its initial public offering priced on Wednesday.  Investors may have been taking the opportunity to lock in some profit: Heading into Friday’s session, shares were up 71% in 2026.

headline: Boeing confirms China commitment to buy 200 aircraft
summary: Aerospace giant Boeing on Friday confirmed that China had committed to purchasing 200 aircraft during a visit to Beijing by US President Donald Trump -- a deal that could ultimately balloon with orders for 750 additional planes.Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One as he flew home from China, Trump said the deal included "a promise of 750 planes, which will be by far the largest order ever, if they do a good job with the 200."

content.title: Boeing confirms China commitment to buy 200 aircraft
content.summary: Aerospace giant Boeing on Friday confirmed that China had committed to purchasing 200 aircraft during a visit to Beijing by US President Donald Trump -- a deal that could ultimately balloon with orders for 750 additional planes.Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One as he flew home from China, Trump said the deal included "a promise of 750 planes, which will be by far the largest order ever, if they do a good job with the 200."

thumbnail.caption: Air China is one of the Chinese airlines that buys aircraft from Boeing (GREG BAKER)

headline: Trump and Boeing say China agreed to buy 200 aircraft, reopening a key market for the US planemaker
summary: Aircraft manufacturer Boeing will make its first major sale to China in nearly a decade under an agreement for 200 planes announced Friday after President Donald Trump’s summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.  Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One as he returned from Beijing, Trump said China also reserved the right to buy as many as 750 Boeing aircraft as part of the deal.  Boeing confirmed the 200-plane order later Friday but did not specify the types of planes or provide any other details.

content.title: Trump and Boeing say China agreed to buy 200 aircraft, reopening a key market for the US planemaker

Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One as he returned from Beijing, Trump said China also reserved the right to buy as many as 750 Boeing aircraft as part of the deal.  Boeing confirmed the 200-plane order later Friday but did not specify the types of planes or provide any other details.
